title: Whole Earth Catalog, Fall 1968
summary: “The Whole Earth Catalog functions as an evaluation and access device. With it, the user should know better what is worth getting and where and how to do the getting.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth 目录系列，核心可读作“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Fall 1968；系列：Whole Earth 目录系列；页数：约 68 页。主题标签：工具。本期的中文读法：工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: Difficult But Possible Supplement to the Whole Earth Catalog, July 1969
summary: “The Supplement corrects and updates information in the most recent Catalog, carries on-going research toward the next Catalog, and publishes what doesn’t fit in the Catalog but does relate to the use of tools.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth 目录系列，核心可读作“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力；战争、技术和公共伦理”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: Difficult But Possible Supplement to the Whole Earth Catalog, September 1970
summary: “The Supplement corrects and updates information in the most recent Catalog, carries on-going research toward the next Catalog, and publishes what doesn’t fit in the Catalog but does relate to the use of tools.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth 目录系列，核心可读作“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力；战争、技术和公共伦理”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：September 1970；系列：Whole Earth 目录系列；页数：约 56 页。主题标签：工具、战争。本期的中文读法：工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力；战争、技术和公共伦理。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力；战争、技术和公共伦理”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: Big Rock Candy Mountain
summary: “The Big Rock Candy Mountain seeks to aid in the acquisition of this knowledge: not by molding the learner into a pre-established pattern, but by providing resources to help him quench his thirst; not by teaching meaningless stockpiling leading to a dissatisfied life, but by encouraging growth in the present leading toward a joyous old age; not by changing people, but by awakening a desire to change. This is our motivation for doing this catalogue.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于专题出版物，核心可读作“自学、教育实验和开放知识；学校之外的学习路径；战争、技术和公共伦理”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
toc: Process Learning: 2 Educational Environments: 10 Classroom Materials: 24 Home Learning: 43 Self Discovery: 48

title: Domebook One
summary: “Domebook One is our attempt at communicating the state of the art of domebuilding, spring 1970. These are domes that we designed, built, and live in; we are thereby testing the ‘physical projection’ of our fantasies.”

title: Difficult But Possible Supplement to the Whole Earth Catalog, January 1971
summary: “The Supplement corrects and updates information in the most recent Catalog, carries on-going research toward the next Catalog, and publishes what doesn’t fit in the Catalog but does relate to the use of tools.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth 目录系列，核心可读作“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力；战争、技术和公共伦理”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：January 1971；系列：Whole Earth 目录系列；页数：约 48 页。主题标签：工具、战争。本期的中文读法：工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力；战争、技术和公共伦理。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力；战争、技术和公共伦理”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: The Last Supplement to The Whole Earth Catalog, March 1971
summary: “What the Catalog has attempted to do is to get out the essential information that is necessary for life on a planet. In many ways the Catalog has succeeded; where the Catalog has failed perhaps the successors it has midwifed, by the early announcement of its intended demise, will someday find the answers required.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth 目录系列，核心可读作“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: The Last Whole Earth Catalog, January 1971
summary: “This issue of the catalog is the last. We encourage others to initiate similar services to fill the vacuum in the economy we stumbled into and are stepping out of.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth 目录系列，核心可读作“自学、教育实验和开放知识；社区组织、互助和地方实践；通信工具与社会组织方式”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
toc: Whole Systems: 3 Land Use: 46 Shelter: 84 Industry: 111 Craft: 146 Community: 177 Nomadics: 245 Communications: 306 Learning: 366 Credits: 434 How to Do a Whole Earth Catalog: 435 Index: 442
toc_zh: 中文线索：学习、社区。原目录题名：Whole Systems: 3 Land Use: 46 Shelter: 84 Industry: 111 Craft: 146 Community: 177 Nomadics: 245
guide_zh: 出版时间：January 1971；系列：Whole Earth 目录系列；页数：约 452 页。主题标签：学习、社区、通信。本期的中文读法：自学、教育实验和开放知识；社区组织、互助和地方实践；通信工具与社会组织方式。阅读顺序建议：先看封面和目录，再围绕这些中文线索进入：学习、社区。

title: Whole Earth Epilog, October 1974
summary: “The Whole Earth Epilog functions as an evaluation and access device. With it, the user should know better what is worth getting and where and how to do the getting.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth 目录系列，核心可读作“自学、教育实验和开放知识；社区组织、互助和地方实践；通信工具与社会组织方式”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
toc: Whole Systems: 452 Land Use: 474 Shelter: 506 Soft Technology: 526 Craft: 546 Community: 576 Nomadics: 634 Communications: 670 Learning: 710 Business: 750 Index: 754
toc_zh: 中文线索：学习、社区。原目录题名：Whole Systems: 452 Land Use: 474 Shelter: 506 Soft Technology: 526 Craft: 546 Community: 576 Nom
guide_zh: 出版时间：October 1974；系列：Whole Earth 目录系列；页数：约 322 页。主题标签：学习、社区、通信。本期的中文读法：自学、教育实验和开放知识；社区组织、互助和地方实践；通信工具与社会组织方式。阅读顺序建议：先看封面和目录，再围绕这些中文线索进入：学习、社区。

title: II Cybernetic Frontiers, January 1974
summary: “Every part of cybernetics research is jumping with fascinating activity. In this book I’m focusing on two frontiers that look particularly poignant and promising. Though polar opposites in their relation to computing machines, they are parallel in their relation to Man’s special pride, conscious purposefulness. Both subvert it.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于专题出版物，核心可读作“计算机如何从专业机器变成个人工具；控制论、反馈和网络社会想象；战争、技术和公共伦理”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Fall 1974
summary: “Emerging from the tumultuous first half decade of the 1960s, the founding of the Black Panther Party by Huey P. Newton and Bobby Seale in October, 1966, marked the beginning of a new era for a tired, troubled and confused America. […] Five simple words seized America’s soul in an attempt to make it whole: ‘ALL POWER TO THE PEOPLE!’”

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Spring 1974
summary: “It seems that all evolution is coevolution. The beauty of the term is what it adds to the concepts of ecology. […] Ecology is whole system alright, but coevolution is whole system in TIME. The health of it is forward— systemic self-education which feeds on constant imperfection. We coevolving watchers and meddlers are not left out of it. Ecology maintains. Coevolution learns.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于共同进化季刊，核心可读作“生态系统、地方知识和长期实践；自学、教育实验和开放知识；学校之外的学习路径”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Summer 1974
summary: “The present world is a much tighter fabric than the 1840’s. Does that make it more stable or less? I estimate that this round it’s less. International repercussions — economic, political, social, military, and spiritual — of the inevitable famines can only be guessed at.”

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Summer 1975
summary: “The Gaia Hypothesis […] treats the anomalous Earth atmosphere as an artifact of life and comprehends the planet itself as a single life. The two old puzzles — 1) How does the bizarre Earth atmosphere maintain itself? and 2) How does fragile Earth life maintain itself? — solve each other.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于共同进化季刊，核心可读作“盖亚假说与整体系统视角”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Fall 1977
summary: “If you’re surprised to learn of all this don’t feel bad. The very firms who used to manufacture solar water heaters (and now make air conditioners and such) were astonished when Butti and Perlin turned up the solar material in their files. title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Spring 1977
summary: “Within the structure of an Arms Race there is no formal solution. They run to exhaustion. The place to look for relief is the context in which they are embedded — the language of the rules of the game and the cultural valuing of its conduct.”

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Summer 1977
summary: “Clearly, then, there is or has been some vitally important element in this observance [of voluntary simplicity]. But the vast quantities of things given to us by modern mass production and commerce, the developments of science and the complexities of existence in modern industrialized countries have raised widespread doubts as to the validity of this practice and principle. Our present ‘mental climate’ is not favorable either to a clear understanding of the value of simplicity or to its practice.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于共同进化季刊，核心可读作“生态系统、地方知识和长期实践”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Summer 1977；系列：共同进化季刊；页数：约 151 页。主题标签：生态。本期的中文读法：生态系统、地方知识和长期实践。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“生态系统、地方知识和长期实践”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Fall 1978
editor: Lawrence Ferlinghetti, Michael McClure, David Meltzer, Gary Snyder
summary: “This version of *Journal For the Protection of All Beings* […] was conceived when Stewart Brand suggested a rebirth of it disguised as a *CoEvolution Quarterly*. We aimed at an issue centered on how to liberate mind & body and protect endangered species (including ourselves) from pathogenic industrial civilization.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于共同进化季刊，核心可读作“战争、技术和公共伦理”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Fall 1978；系列：共同进化季刊；页数：约 149 页。主题标签：战争。本期的中文读法：战争、技术和公共伦理。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“战争、技术和公共伦理”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Spring 1978
summary: “The evolution of language as an instrument of play and amusement probably occurred parallel to (or even preceded) its evolution as a practical tool. There is no question, at any rate, that story-telling is an ancient art, and that like most play it serves a vital — if often unrecognized — function, for the values and underlying principles of any culture have always been most effectively carried from one generation to the next via the medium of its stories, myths and legends.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于共同进化季刊，核心可读作“生态系统、地方知识和长期实践”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Spring 1978；系列：共同进化季刊；页数：约 157 页。主题标签：生态。本期的中文读法：生态系统、地方知识和长期实践。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“生态系统、地方知识和长期实践”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

summary: “But this Great Book, the *Whole Earth Catalog* had more pages on achieving Mokja than on getting to the beach, which we always figured was the same thing. title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Summer 1979
summary: “Plant a tree — is there any more certain an undubious good? Good for the soil, good for the watershed, good for the atmosphere (trees eat CO2), good for the shade, good for the fruit, the firewood, the lumber, good for the aesthetics […], good for the continuity, good for climbing and tree houses, a cheap, easy, enjoyable investment with non-stop rewards.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于共同进化季刊，核心可读作“土壤、农业和修复性实践；水资源、环境和基础设施；火、能源和风险治理”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Summer 1979；系列：共同进化季刊；页数：约 151 页。主题标签：土壤、水、火、战争。本期的中文读法：土壤、农业和修复性实践；水资源、环境和基础设施；火、能源和风险治理。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“土壤、农业和修复性实践；水资源、环境和基础设施；火、能源和风险治理”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Winter 1979
summary: “What do we do with a person who slowly tortures children to death? That is what Factories are doing, not only to the bodies and minds of children, but all human beings and all living things on earth. Faster and faster the air we breathe, water we drink, food we eat is poisoned by Factories.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于共同进化季刊，核心可读作“食物生产、饮食和生态关系；水资源、环境和基础设施”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Spring 1980
summary: “Our history provides us with a wide range of possibilities in transportation — dirigibles, luxurious passenger trains, locally-owned shortline railroads, coastwise shipping, simpler automobiles, stagecoach lines, and neighborhood bus lines. None of these tools are fantastic new inventions that may or may not be possible but real things that were once taken for granted.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于共同进化季刊，核心可读作“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Spring 1980；系列：共同进化季刊；页数：约 153 页。主题标签：工具。本期的中文读法：工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Summer 1980
summary: “In all manner of gaits, people ran as a means of travel, communication, transportation, ceremony and competition. Training and attention to running taboos were essential in the preparedness for hunting, warfare, ritual or sport — purposes frequently intertwined.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于共同进化季刊，核心可读作“战争、技术和公共伦理；通信工具与社会组织方式”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Summer 1980；系列：共同进化季刊；页数：约 153 页。主题标签：战争、通信。本期的中文读法：战争、技术和公共伦理；通信工具与社会组织方式。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“战争、技术和公共伦理；通信工具与社会组织方式”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Winter 1980
summary: “If you can understand trees, you can understand what’s happening all over the world. It’s quite unnecessary to chop them down, and pulp them, and mangle them, and then squirt squid juice all over them to understand anything. Nature is God’s Groupie.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于共同进化季刊，核心可读作“生态系统、地方知识和长期实践”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Winter 1980；系列：共同进化季刊；页数：约 151 页。主题标签：生态。本期的中文读法：生态系统、地方知识和长期实践。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“生态系统、地方知识和长期实践”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Fall 1981
summary: “The Four Corners project is the construction of the world’s largest sculpture using the least amount of material. It consists of an invisible tetrahedron spanning the inside of the Earth with the outer four corners just protruding from the crust of the Earth.”

title: CoEvolution Catalog, October 1982
summary: “We're trying something here — an article commissioned strictly for use in a promotional mailing. The shorter deadline-to-delivery delay gives the writer a shot at unusual timeliness. Because of everyone’s interest in the economy these days, Hawken is the most popular contributor in *CoEvolution*’s history. In April 1983 he goes mainstream with publication by Holt, Rinehart of his book *The Next Economy* as their major spring title.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于共同进化季刊，核心可读作“生态系统、地方知识和长期实践”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：October 1982；系列：共同进化季刊；页数：约 17 页。主题标签：生态。本期的中文读法：生态系统、地方知识和长期实践。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“生态系统、地方知识和长期实践”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Fall 1983
summary: “This account is not the one-eyed view of exposé journalism. Nor the one-eyed view of an embittered true believer. All life with eyes that isn’t maimed sees with a minimum of two eyes. Joseph Stevenson reports that way on an exceptionally worthy activity that has slipped a bit, not too far for easy repair. This too is love, Mother Teresa. Maintenance and improvement is as inspirational as founding (especially these slipshod days), or what is the Church for?”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于共同进化季刊，核心可读作“生态系统、地方知识和长期实践”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Fall 1983；系列：共同进化季刊；页数：约 149 页。主题标签：生态。本期的中文读法：生态系统、地方知识和长期实践。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“生态系统、地方知识和长期实践”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Spring 1983
summary: “Africa is an old and mellow piece of crust. As opposed to North America, you don’t have to learn about endless oceanic invasions of the land nor glaciers tearing apart old soils and creating and recreating the shape of the continent. Africa, as a tectonic place, has had few upheavals.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于共同进化季刊，核心可读作“地点、土地和真实生活场景；土壤、农业和修复性实践”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Spring 1983；系列：共同进化季刊；页数：约 165 页。主题标签：地方、土壤。本期的中文读法：地点、土地和真实生活场景；土壤、农业和修复性实践。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“地点、土地和真实生活场景；土壤、农业和修复性实践”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Summer 1983
summary: “The purpose of this article is to review the evidence for Gaia and to present a simple model, called ‘Daisy World,’ specifically developed to show that global homeostasis does. not need foresight or planning by life.”

title: CoEvolution Quarterly, Fall 1984
summary: “I’d seen our sun moving down behind the stationary horizon of an apparently fixed world. Though I knew that the earth spun, I did not see or feel it. The world I experienced was passive and immensely unmoving. But a simple shift in interpretation, from the horizon as stationary to the sun as stationary, caused the horizon and my frame of reference to tilt up in front of the sun.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于共同进化季刊，核心可读作“生态系统、地方知识和长期实践”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: Whole Earth Software Catalog, June 1984
summary: “For new computer users these days the most daunting task is not learning how to use the machine but shopping. Hence this book. The impossible (and unachieved) task of the Whole Earth Software Catalog is to identify and comparatively describe all of the best personal computer products—especially software, where the most confusion reigns.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于软件目录与评论，核心可读作“计算机如何从专业机器变成个人工具；软件如何塑造新的工作、学习和审美实践；自学、教育实验和开放知识”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: Whole Earth Software Review No.1, Spring 1984
summary: “This magazine is a book-in-progress. The book is the Whole Earth Software Catalog, first edition to appear late in 1984, published by Doubleday. Its subject is everything having to do with personal computers. Its function is to evaluate the best of what’s available for personal computers.”

summary: “One of the subtle advantages—you appear as a mind to others… A very liberating environment where you bump into electronically-linked communities of people you didn't know were out there. title: Whole Earth Software Review No.3, Fall 1984
summary: “Software has new versions all the time; why can’t a book?”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于软件目录与评论，核心可读作“软件如何塑造新的工作、学习和审美实践；战争、技术和公共伦理”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Fall 1984；系列：软件目录与评论；页数：约 137 页。主题标签：软件、战争。本期的中文读法：软件如何塑造新的工作、学习和审美实践；战争、技术和公共伦理。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“软件如何塑造新的工作、学习和审美实践；战争、技术和公共伦理”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: Whole Earth Review, Fall 1985
summary: “Urban legends are bizarre but believed stories; traditional (i.e., fictional) accounts, but told as true. They are often quite plausible tales, but also too good to be true. They supposedly describe things that have happened to a friend-of-a-friend, the source that folklore scholars call a FOAF.”

title: Whole Earth Review, Winter 1985
summary: “The original idea behind this Special Section was to present material on Islam that could provide a glimpse of its attractions. What is it about this major world religion that motivates such fervent enthusiasm among some adherents? In search of insights we contacted people — readers of *WER*, in many cases — who had converted to Islam or spent years in its orbit and asked them to help us see Islam through their eyes. Most other media are primed to present Islam as a Problem; we were curious about Islam as a Solution.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Review，核心可读作“软件如何塑造新的工作、学习和审美实践；战争、技术和公共伦理”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: Whole Earth Review, Fall 1986
summary: “If modern myths are cultural expressions, then their creators and adherents are social experimenters — however misguided, erratic, or uneducated — who are attempting to bridge that chasm between the visionary and the scientific in order to build a new unifying myth. At worst, they show us dead ends that we need not traverse again. At best, they provide us with clues, puzzle pieces for our Future Collective Myth, if such a thing is to be.”

title: The Essential Whole Earth Catalog, September 1986
summary: “A realm of intimate, personal power is developing—the power of individuals to conduct their own education, find their own inspiration, shape their own environment, and share the adventure with whoever is interested. Tools that aid this process are sought and promoted by The Essential Whole Earth Catalog”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth 目录系列，核心可读作“自学、教育实验和开放知识；学校之外的学习路径；工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: 10 Years of CoEvolution Quarterly: News That Stayed News, 1986
summary: “In this volume are examples of reporting and storytelling that truly meet Ezra Pound’s definition of literature: ‘news that stays news.’ ‘Every day of the five years I worked (as editor of CoEvolution Quarterly),’ writes Art Kleiner, coeditor of this collection, ‘I was conscious that we were printing material that could last long beyond the lifespan of a typical magazine issue… Here is the best of that material.’”

title: Whole Earth Review, Fall 1988
summary: “Evolution is full of surprised. I don’t mean the funny-shaped animals, like flounder or angler fish, that readily evoke an exclamation or laugh. I’m referring to the improbable ways that evolution has of doing things, the surprising paths taken by evolution that violate all the stereotyped notions about progress. Sometimes evolution isn’t the slow grind, meandering along, continuously editing random variations into ever-better versions.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Review，核心可读作“工具文化”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: Signal: Communication Tools for the Information Age
summary: “This book limits its investigation to all possible tools having information at one end and a grasp at the other. It considers any idea that can amplify the reach of personal communication, any science which can intercept the power of larger circuits to redirect its flow toward individuals, and any art that can nurture data into information, information into knowledge, and knowledge into wisdom.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth 目录系列，核心可读作“网络连接怎样改变协作和知识流动；工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力；战争、技术和公共伦理”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
toc: Prime Information: 4 The Order of Languages: 20 Publishing Frontiers: 40 Network Societies: 69 Bodily Communication: 100 The Audible Signal: 115 Visual Knowledge: 136 Digital Thinking: 163 Information Civics: 182 Mind Circuits: 202
toc_zh: 中文线索：网络。原目录题名：Prime Information: 4 The Order of Languages: 20 Publishing Frontiers: 40 Network Societies: 69 B
guide_zh: 出版时间：1988；系列：Whole Earth 目录系列；页数：约 236 页。主题标签：网络、工具、战争、通信。本期的中文读法：网络连接怎样改变协作和知识流动；工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力；战争、技术和公共伦理。阅读顺序建议：先看封面和目录，再围绕这些中文线索进入：网络。

title: Whole Earth Review, Summer 1989
summary: “I enjoy technology that augments my body — the computer upon which this essay was typed, the backpack that gives me the freedom of the mountains — but I don’t see that technology as replacing the body. There's a saying: ‘If it ain't broke, don’t fix it.’ The body is a near-miraculous organism of unsurpassed versatility, flexibility, and creativity. What machine can begin to replace all that? And why would we want to?”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Review，核心可读作“计算机如何从专业机器变成个人工具；地点、土地和真实生活场景”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Summer 1989；系列：Whole Earth Review；页数：约 149 页。主题标签：计算机、地方。本期的中文读法：计算机如何从专业机器变成个人工具；地点、土地和真实生活场景。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“计算机如何从专业机器变成个人工具；地点、土地和真实生活场景”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: Whole Earth Review, Winter 1989
summary: “By some time in the mid-1990s, over half the humans on earth will be under the age of 20. Along the way these billions of teenagers will listen to the same music, watch the same movies, wear the same clothes, and perhaps study the same things in school. There is a Global Teenager emerging, global in both proportion and perspective.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Review，核心可读作“工具文化”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: The Fringes of Reason: Whole Earth Catalog
summary: “This is not a book about Orthodoxy. Instead, this is a book about tinkerers who spend their lives in basements perfecting perpetual motion machines, contactees who converse on a first-name basis with entities from outer space, and reincarnationists who remember past lives as royalty on the lost continent of Atlantis. This is, in fact, a book about people so fed up with Orthodoxy and the mundane bit part it handed them that they did something about it. Something imaginative. Something colorful. Something… different.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth 目录系列，核心可读作“战争、技术和公共伦理”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
toc: Forward: 3 Introduction: 6 The New, Improved Age: 7 Inner Frontiers: 53 Everything You Know is Wrong!: 77 Weird Science: 101 Not of This Earth: 137 What is Reality?: 169 Index: 220

title: Whole Earth Review, Fall 1990
summary: “What is the epistemology of a disaster? In other words, what is the nature of our responses to a disaster — who will respond in what ways, based on what factors? What knowledge can be derived so as to better prepare us for future disasters?”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Review，核心可读作“未来想象与可操作的改变”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Fall 1990；系列：Whole Earth Review；页数：约 147 页。主题标签：未来。本期的中文读法：未来想象与可操作的改变。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“未来想象与可操作的改变”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: Whole Earth Review, Spring 1990
summary: “This issue is devoted to environmental restoration. Consider it an invitation to get your hands dirty. Old houses can be restored, but so can streams, marshes, and forests. Here are people working with nature, explaining how they do it and why. As an activity that invites your participation, environmental restoration is a way of focusing on the particular instead of the planet.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Review，核心可读作“工具文化”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: Whole Earth Review, Summer 1990
summary: “This issue never set out to have a theme. It began as a serendipitous catch of all that flows through the office, particularly of the many product, book, and catalog reviews piling up on our shelves. Indeed, there are more reviews than usual in this issue. But it is only now, the last week in production, that an alliance among the ideas on these pages has emerged. What vou have here is an issue pointing to the reality of autonomous worlds. […] In short, welcome to the second Creation, the age of Synthetic Realities.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Review，核心可读作“工具文化”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: Whole Earth Review, Winter 1990
summary: “This is the WHOLE Earth Review, and that includes countries other than the United States. The following pages present a mosaic of viewpoints from American and Japanese writers. The idea is to get away from the quest for easy answers and get started on some of the hard questions. The questions raised here can be used as tools for understanding some of the issues that separate American and Japanese interests, as maps to some of the cultural bridges that exist now, and as pointers to the places where bridges are waiting to be built.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Review，核心可读作“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力；地点、土地和真实生活场景”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Winter 1990；系列：Whole Earth Review；页数：约 149 页。主题标签：工具、地方。本期的中文读法：工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力；地点、土地和真实生活场景。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力；地点、土地和真实生活场景”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: Whole Earth Review, Spring 1991
summary: “Even if you are not interested in technology — especially if you are not interested in technology — I urge you to read this issue’s lead article, ‘Highways of the Mind.’ Legislators and business executives are making decisions now about the construction of a nationwide information and communication network that will affect the way we get news, do business, educate our youth, and conduct scientific, medical, and scholarly research. If citizens hope to influence these long-range, high-impact decisions, we need a lot more knowledge about this technology and its implications than we have now.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Review，核心可读作“网络连接怎样改变协作和知识流动；通信工具与社会组织方式”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: Whole Earth Review, Winter 1991
summary: “Modern technology makes millions of people as rich as sultans at the expense of many more millions of other people who live near some of the resources that must be extracted from the Earth in order for the whole system to continue to produce profits. The system feeds on the Earth itself, and is addicted to growth. Sounds cancerous, doesn’t it?”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Review，核心可读作“工具文化”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: Helping Nature Heal, Spring 1991
summary: “A person turns her back by putting a seedling in the earth with her two hands. A person turns his back by firing prairie grass. You turn your back by rediscovering the plants and migratory birds of your own back yard. We turn our backs by joining in the hard, common, physical labor of shoveling, seeding, heaving, portaging, firing, transplanting, and channeling to restore disturbed and abused land to a semblance of its original, complex, graceful coherence.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于专题出版物，核心可读作“围绕单一主题展开的特别册或专题书”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

summary: “The eventual benefits of artificial life are limited only by imagination: self-reproducing factories, microscopic disease-killing machines, a menagerie of lifeforms performing useful functions.”

title: Whole Earth Review, Spring 1992
summary: “I have been an activist for twenty years and a witch for much longer. | have grown old trying to convince and teach feminist and mainstream women that spirituality and reverence for the Goddess would only enhance their labors and, in fact, fuel them when they feel depleted by too much political work. Resource/energy management, I argued, is a spiritual skill.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Review，核心可读作“能源选择与日常生活技术；女性经验、劳动与社会结构”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Spring 1992；系列：Whole Earth Review；页数：约 147 页。主题标签：能源、女性。本期的中文读法：能源选择与日常生活技术；女性经验、劳动与社会结构。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“能源选择与日常生活技术；女性经验、劳动与社会结构”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: Whole Earth Review, Fall 1993
summary: “If you are fortunate enough to share a neighborhood with a leafy elm, a gnarly oak, a soaring redwood, take another look at its silhouette against the sky. That self-similar 4-D explosion of branching branches is a clue to a cosmic riddle or two, and a key concept in fields as unrelated as vascular surgery and software design.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Review，核心可读作“软件如何塑造新的工作、学习和审美实践；设计作为工具、环境和生活方式的组织方法；战争、技术和公共伦理”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: Whole Earth Review, Spring 1993
summary: “The section called Real-Life Women is the purest distillation of this notion, the voices of women (most of them not professional writers) I happened to hear, online or at lunch or in response to a call for contributions published in the last issue of WER.”

guide_zh: 出版时间：Spring 1993；系列：Whole Earth Review；页数：约 149 页。主题标签：女性。本期的中文读法：女性经验、劳动与社会结构。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“女性经验、劳动与社会结构”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: Whole Earth Review, Summer 1993
summary: “This issue is not about Whole Earth and its glorious history, nor about What Went Wrong and What Went Right With the Sixties (though I was tempted), nor about The Great Changes 25 Years Have Wrought, nor even about The Next 25 Years. […] But mostly this issue is just a collection of what I found most interesting and available in the winter of 1992-93. The organizers of the issue are the times and my peculiar curiosity responding to the times.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Review，核心可读作“工具文化”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: Whole Earth Review, Winter 1993
summary: “Silence, fear, and shame are allies of sexual abusers. We need to talk about protecting our children; we need to talk about the realities of sexual abuse. We also need to talk about protecting the rights of citizens in the face of accusations that can ruin reputations and wreck lives. The courts, law enforcement agencies, schools, and families need to bring into the light that which has grown powerful in the darkness and silence.”

title: Whole Earth Review, Summer 1994
summary: “Running in place at the speed of light, we defensively cling to our unexamined notions of categories, our dilapidated signposts in a bleak landscape. They make things simple again. Reflections of control, they reassure us that there’s a time and a place for everything. […] They are easy systems. Use them, but doubt them. They are the rules of the game, but perhaps no longer the one being played.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Review，核心可读作“地点、土地和真实生活场景”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: The Millennium Whole Earth Catalog, December 1994
summary: “The Millennium Whole Earth Catalog integrates the best tools from the past twenty-five years with the best tools for the next twenty-five years, including old Whole Earth Catalog standards such as environmental restoration, community-building, whole systems thinking, and medical self-care as well as new categories of resources such as backyard biodiversity, electronic mail, eco-tourism, and green investing.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth 目录系列，核心可读作“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力；社区组织、互助和地方实践；绿色运动的工具、伦理和实践”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：December 1994；系列：Whole Earth 目录系列；页数：约 412 页。主题标签：工具、社区、绿色运动、建造。本期的中文读法：工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力；社区组织、互助和地方实践；绿色运动的工具、伦理和实践。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力；社区组织、互助和地方实践；绿色运动的工具、伦理和实践”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: Whole Earth Review, Fall 1995
summary: “War is a hard topic for rational thought. That's not to say that nobody reasons about its methods or its history; there are whole industries for that. […] But what seems to be missing, or at least hard to come by, is a rationale for reacting to a war, a structure for valuing it, comparing it to other wars, and deciding — as I think we have a responsibility to decide — whether a war's costs will be balanced by its justifications.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Review，核心可读作“战争、技术和公共伦理”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Fall 1995；系列：Whole Earth Review；页数：约 133 页。主题标签：战争。本期的中文读法：战争、技术和公共伦理。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“战争、技术和公共伦理”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: Whole Earth Review, Summer 1995
summary: “Everybody from the president to the peanut vendor talks about prosperity. Are we on the path to it? Have we lost it? Will it be there for our children’s children? Prosperity is the main promise of the American Dream. Its absence is called ‘downward mobility.’ But no one seems to know what it is.”

summary: “Over the last quarter-century, we’ve learned that access to tools and ideas is not enough. Practice and experience refine handwork and wisdom, make it better, secure a more subtle touch. So, we’ve changed our motto from ‘access to tools and ideas’ to ‘access to ideas, tools, and practices.’”

title: Whole Earth, Winter 1997
summary: “Since Earth Day, prophets have propagated like lap tops. Believers and non-believers hear doom-gloom Jeremiahs, techno-Pollyannas, and Coyote forecasters of a time, not too far off, when all things will be reconnected. Earthlove could be the wildcard.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Magazine，核心可读作“专题化的生态”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Winter 1997；系列：Whole Earth Magazine；页数：约 109 页。主题标签：专题化的生态。本期的中文读法：专题化的生态。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“专题化的生态”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: Whole Earth Catalog 30th Anniversary, Winter 1998
summary: “To celebrate, we have combined the first Catalog with the winter issue of Whole Earth (the magazine some still call CoEvolution Quarterly). Here are about forty short essays by friends. It’s a continuation of the old theme: what’s a difference that might most make a difference, a difference close to your heart or mind?”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth 目录系列，核心可读作“设计作为工具、环境和生活方式的组织方法；自学、教育实验和开放知识”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
toc: 1968 Whole Earth Catalog: 5 Dancing Patterns: [2, 69] Learning: [6, 73] Plants: [12, 79] Animals: [15, 84] Paths: [16, 85] Making Stuff: [20, 89] Wilderness & Civilization: [22, 91] Designing/Restoring: [29, 101] Beauty: [34, 103]
toc_zh: 中文线索：设计、学习。原目录题名：1968 Whole Earth Catalog: 5 Dancing Patterns: [2, 69] Learning: [6, 73] Plants: [12, 79] Animals
guide_zh: 出版时间：Winter 1998；系列：Whole Earth 目录系列；页数：约 108 页。主题标签：设计、学习。本期的中文读法：设计作为工具、环境和生活方式的组织方法；自学、教育实验和开放知识。阅读顺序建议：先看封面和目录，再围绕这些中文线索进入：设计、学习。

title: Whole Earth, Fall 1999
summary: “The Gaia hypothesis, developed by James Lovelock and Lynn Margulis, asserts that this planet is alive. This integrates a vast amount of information in a single image: What we are talking about is life. It wiggles. It may bite. The Gaian metaphor provides a bridge from high technical specificity to all the experiences that go with direct contact with a living being.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Magazine，核心可读作“盖亚假说与整体系统视角”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: Whole Earth, Spring 1999
summary: “Soil as the mother of all things; soil as the strength that gives us groundedness; soil as receptive and creative producer of foods and fibers; soil as the anchor for trees; soil as pigment to decorate our bodies and canvasses; soil as purifier of dirty waters; soil as medicine; soil as building blocks for homes and roads; soil as the natural capital most undervalued on the planet; soil as the teacher of virtues and the stuff of myth.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Magazine，核心可读作“食物生产、饮食和生态关系；土壤、农业和修复性实践；水资源、环境和基础设施”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Spring 1999；系列：Whole Earth Magazine；页数：约 125 页。主题标签：食物、土壤、水、建造。本期的中文读法：食物生产、饮食和生态关系；土壤、农业和修复性实践；水资源、环境和基础设施。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“食物生产、饮食和生态关系；土壤、农业和修复性实践；水资源、环境和基础设施”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: Whole Earth, Summer 2000
summary: “I’d explore one group’s convictions, granting them the benefit of the doubt, and see how it felt to see the world through their eyes. In due course, I’d do the same with the next, and the next. The result of this exercise […] was a kind of self-deprogramming, wherein terms like “left” or “right” simply lost their negative or positive charge.”

title: Whole Earth, Winter 2000
summary: “This issue has been filled from books and tools around his home — ‘discovered’ by taking a moment and perusing the household shelves; maybe poking around in closets and drawers, and saying ‘these excite me at this moment of life.’ The items are idiosyncratic; not an attempt at a comprehensive selection as might appear in a Whole Earth Catalog.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Magazine，核心可读作“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Winter 2000；系列：Whole Earth Magazine；页数：约 103 页。主题标签：工具。本期的中文读法：工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“工具如何扩大个人和社区的行动能力”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: Whole Earth, Spring 2001
summary: “Place making has entered the mainstream and some mainstream constituencies have rebelled. Who cares if landscapes are all dandelions and lawn? Why do restorers get to create their version of nature? Place making and culture shaping entangle. A ten-year update on restoring nature to neighborhoods, watersheds, and intercontinental migration routes. A world of rich ambiguity. What’s possible? practical? sustainable?”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Magazine，核心可读作“地点、土地和真实生活场景；水资源、环境和基础设施”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
guide_zh: 出版时间：Spring 2001；系列：Whole Earth Magazine；页数：约 109 页。主题标签：地方、水。本期的中文读法：地点、土地和真实生活场景；水资源、环境和基础设施。阅读顺序建议：先把它看作一张关于“地点、土地和真实生活场景；水资源、环境和基础设施”的时代切片，再打开原扫描核对图片、版式和语境。

title: Whole Earth, Summer 2001
summary: “In this issue, Whole Earth has finally been hoist on its digital petard. The Viridian Design Movement is a virtual community of high-tech greens. We have burst out of our customary nexus of lists and websites, and taken over the magazine.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Magazine，核心可读作“设计作为工具、环境和生活方式的组织方法；社区组织、互助和地方实践；绿色运动的工具、伦理和实践”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
toc: Introduction: 4 Grand Schemes: 7 Texas Green: 19 Think Tanks: 28 The Literary View: 35 Technology, the Bitch Goddess: 40 The Viridian View of Design: 49 Futurefeedforward: [73, 76] Design is Not Art: [77, 80] Being Green in 2001 AD: [84, 87] Backmatter: [91, 94]
toc_zh: 中文线索：设计、未来。原目录题名：Introduction: 4 Grand Schemes: 7 Texas Green: 19 Think Tanks: 28 The Literary View: 35 Technolog
guide_zh: 出版时间：Summer 2001；系列：Whole Earth Magazine；页数：约 107 页。主题标签：设计、社区、绿色运动、未来。本期的中文读法：设计作为工具、环境和生活方式的组织方法；社区组织、互助和地方实践；绿色运动的工具、伦理和实践。阅读顺序建议：先看封面和目录，再围绕这些中文线索进入：设计、未来。

summary: “A transformation of the Energy Web is under way. Local power has become less costly, more reliable, and more secure. summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Magazine，核心可读作“能源选择与日常生活技术；地点、土地和真实生活场景”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。

title: Whole Earth, Fall 2002
summary: “The desire for trusting relations and a secure social environment, and the anxiety and fear that accompany that distrust and insecurity, are fundamental not only for humans but for all social mammals. Our ancestors undoubtedly cared about ‘social security’ long before we became fully human. By studying other animals perhaps we can gain insights useful in our current efforts to create a more secure world for ourselves and other species.”
summary_zh: 中文摘要：本期属于Whole Earth Magazine，核心可读作“专题化的生态”。它的价值不是单篇知识点，而是把工具、实践经验、图片和时代议题组织成一张可漫游的阅读地图。
